name: negedge_always_ff_register
description: Code should include an always_ff block triggered on negedge clock with non-blocking assignment to a register. This triggers a Yosys optimization bug where the register receives an incorrect (zero) value instead of the assigned literal value.

name: negedge_nonblocking_literal_assignment
description: Code should include a non-blocking assignment of a multi-bit literal constant (7'b0011111) to a reg variable inside an always_ff block triggered by negedge clock. This exposes a synthesis optimization bug where the literal value is corrupted to zero.

name: negedge_clock_initial_high
description: Code should include a clock signal initialized to 1 and toggled via procedural assignment in an initial block to trigger a negedge event. This combined with always_ff negedge trigger exposes the Yosys bug.
snippet:
reg clk = 1;
initial begin
    #1 clk = 0;  // negedge trigger
end
